perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to use all reasonable precautions, no less than Recipient’s treatment of its own Confidential Information of a similar nature, to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the Company’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to use reasonable precautions, but is not responsible for security breaches if they are caused by factors beyond their direct control, to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the Company’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception where Recipient is not responsible for security breaches beyond their direct control. This contradicts the more absolute terms in Section 2, where Recipient 'agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence'. The phrase: 'agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence' implies a stricter obligation, and the introduced perturbation creates an exception in contrast to the general obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees with a need- to- know for the contemplated purpose set forth above, and shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any party who is not bound by a non-disclosure agreement with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Recipient agrees to use reasonable precautions, while the changed text creates an exception relieving Recipient from responsibility for security breaches beyond their control. This change directly contradicts the contradicted text's statement that 'Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence,' which implies a strict obligation without exceptions. Therefore, the change introduces a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information, regardless of whether it is in oral or written, or partial or complete, form, shall be and remain the property of the Company, whether provided to or copied by Recipient. Recipient shall not make or have made any partial or complete copies or derivative works, whether in hand copy or electronic form, of any of the Confidential Information without the express prior authorization of the Company, and any legends or notices used by the Company in the Confidential Information shall be reproduced in full in all copies. Upon demand by the Company or termination of this Agreement, all materials bearing or disclosing any of the Confidential Information shall be delivered or returned to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information, regardless of whether it is in oral or written, or partial or complete, form, shall be and remain the property of the Company, whether provided to or copied by Recipient. Recipient may make partial or complete copies or derivative works, whether in hand copy or electronic form, of any of the Confidential Information for internal use, and any legends or notices used by the Company in the Confidential Information shall be reproduced in full in all copies. Upon demand by the Company or termination of this Agreement, all materials bearing or disclosing any of the Confidential Information shall be delivered or returned to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation alters Section 4 to permit the Recipient to make copies for 'internal use'. This contradicts Section 2, where Recipient agrees that Confidential Information 'shall not be used for any other purpose' than the originally 'contemplated purpose set forth above'. The permission to make copies for internal use expands the usage beyond the initially 'contemplated purpose', creating a conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees with a need- to- know for the contemplated purpose set forth above, and shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any party who is not bound by a non-disclosure agreement with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'Recipient shall not make or have made any partial or complete copies or derivative works'. The changed text modifies this to allow the Recipient to 'make partial or complete copies or derivative works, whether in hand copy or electronic form, of any of the Confidential Information for internal use'. The contradicted text states 'Recipient agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees with a need- to- know for the contemplated purpose set forth above, and shall not be used for any other purpose'. The permission to make copies for 'internal use' directly conflicts with the restriction that the information 'shall not be used for any other purpose' than the originally contemplated purpose.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for as long as the Confidential Information remains confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for five (5) years from the Effective Date, regardless of whether the Confidential Information remains confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the agreement's duration to five years, regardless of the information's confidential nature. This contradicts the original clause that states the agreement remains in effect 'for as long as the Confidential Information remains confidential'. Section 8 acknowledges that damages alone are not an adequate remedy, implying that the confidentiality should be maintained indefinitely, and the introduced perturbation, directly conflicts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that damages alone would not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it of the provisions of this Agreement and, accordingly, without prejudice to any and all other rights and remedies that the Company might have, the Company shall be entitled without proof of special damage to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other -3- equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this Agreement without the requirement of posting a bond. Recipient agrees that the Company shall be entitled to an award of its reasonable attorneys’ fees, expert fees and costs if it prevails in any action to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that the agreement's obligations would persist as long as the Confidential Information remained confidential. The changed text, however, limits the agreement's duration to five years, regardless of the information's confidential nature. This directly contradicts the original clause and conflicts with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text acknowledges that damages alone are insufficient, suggesting indefinite confidentiality. The changed text introduces a definitive endpoint, creating a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction because the five year term creates an inconsistency with the indefinite protection of the confidential information, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose the Company’s Confidential Information if and only to the extent that a disclosure is required by applicable law, provided that Recipient uses reasonable efforts to limit the disclosure by means of a protective order or a request for Confidential/Attorneys’ Eyes Only treatment that permits the Company a reasonable opportunity to review the proposed disclosure before it is made, and to interpose its own objection to the disclosure and, if appropriate, bring a motion for a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose the Company’s Confidential Information if and only to the extent that a disclosure is required by applicable law, and Recipient is required to notify the Company within 24 hours of any such disclosure. Recipient is not required to seek a protective order or consent from the Company for such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement for the Recipient to seek a protective order or allow the Company to review the proposed disclosure. This change directly contradicts the clause in Section 2 that requires specific authorization from the Company for any disclosure unless specifically authorized by this Agreement, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. Recipient agrees to hold all of the Confidential Information in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only by Recipient or its employees with a need- to- know for the contemplated purpose set forth above, and shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any party who is not bound by a non-disclosure agreement with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Recipient to seek a protective order or allow the Company to review the proposed disclosure when disclosure is required by applicable law. However, the contradicted text explicitly states that the Recipient may not disclose Confidential Information to any third party, except as specifically authorized by this Agreement or as specifically authorized by the Company in writing. The change allows disclosure without company authorization, contradicting the earlier section.
